calendar.description: House Law Enforcement and Criminal Justice Hearing
Senate Economic Development and Workforce Services Hearing

general_provisions: This bill provides amendments to school safety standards regarding requirements for various safety related personnel.
hilighted_provisions: This bill:<hr><ltbullet>allows county security chiefs to designate approved private security companies to administer required school safety personnel training;<hr><ltbullet>requires the State Board of Education to administer stipends for a school guardian in coordination with the state security chief; <hr><ltbullet>requires a local education agency (LEA) to provide a panic alert device for certain school safety personnel; <hr><ltbullet>expands the definition of an armed school security guard to include a special function officer; <hr><ltbullet>clarifies requirements of a school guardian concealed carrying a firearm while on duty;<hr><ltbullet>prohibits certain devices in schools;directs the Cybersecurity Commission to make rules establishing minimum cybersecurity standards for local education agencies (LEAs) aligned with industry recognized frameworks;<hr><ltbullet>establishes a phased implementation timeline for LEA compliance;<hr><ltbullet>requires coordination among the Utah Cyber Center, the State Board of Education, and the Utah Education and Telehealth Network;<hr><ltbullet>establishes reporting requirements for cybersecurity incidents;<hr><ltbullet>requires the State Board of Education to provide implementation support and resources;<hr><ltbullet>includes a coordination clause to incorporate changes made to Section 53G-7-227 with changes made to that section in S.B. 69, School Device Revisions;<hr><ltbullet>provides protocols for a school guardian or armed school security guard for an incident using deadly force;<hr><ltbullet>establishes visitor management protocols for an LEA;<hr><ltbullet>removes duplicative language; and<hr><ltbullet>makes conforming changes.
